session.c(3) Library Functions Manual session.c(3)

NAME

session.c

SYNOPSIS

Functions


discovery_session_t * discovery_session_create (const discovery_config_t *config)
Create a new discovery session. void discovery_session_destroy (discovery_session_t *session)
Destroy discovery session and free resources. asciichat_error_t discovery_session_start (discovery_session_t *session)
Start the discovery session. asciichat_error_t discovery_session_process (discovery_session_t *session, int64_t timeout_ns)
Process session events (call in main loop). void discovery_session_stop (discovery_session_t *session)
Stop the discovery session. discovery_state_t discovery_session_get_state (const discovery_session_t *session)
Get current session state. bool discovery_session_is_active (const discovery_session_t *session)
Check if session is active (call in progress). const char * discovery_session_get_string (const discovery_session_t *session)
Get session string. bool discovery_session_is_host (const discovery_session_t *session)
Check if we are the host. session_host_t * discovery_session_get_host (discovery_session_t *session)
Get host context (if we are host). session_participant_t * discovery_session_get_participant (discovery_session_t *session)
Get participant context (if we are participant). asciichat_error_t discovery_session_init_ring (discovery_session_t *session)
Initialize ring consensus state. asciichat_error_t discovery_session_start_ring_round (discovery_session_t *session)
Start a new ring consensus round (every 5 minutes or on new joiner). asciichat_error_t discovery_session_check_host_alive (discovery_session_t *session)
Detect host disconnect (check connection status). asciichat_error_t discovery_session_handle_host_disconnect (discovery_session_t *session, uint32_t disconnect_reason)
Handle host disconnect with automatic failover to future host. asciichat_error_t discovery_session_become_host (discovery_session_t *session)
Become the host (called when elected as future host). asciichat_error_t discovery_session_connect_to_future_host (discovery_session_t *session)
Connect to pre-elected future host (called when NOT future host). asciichat_error_t discovery_session_get_future_host (const discovery_session_t *session, uint8_t out_id[16], char out_address[64], uint16_t *out_port, uint8_t *out_connection_type)
Get future host information. bool discovery_session_is_future_host (const discovery_session_t *session)
Check if we are the future host.

Function Documentation

asciichat_error_t discovery_session_become_host (discovery_session_t * session)

Become the host (called when elected as future host).

Parameters

Session Session context

Returns

ASCIICHAT_OK on success

Definition at line 2364 of file src/discovery/session.c.

2364                                                                               {
2365   if (!session) {
2366     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session is NULL");
2367     return ERROR_INVALID_PARAM;
2368   }
2369 
2370   log_info("Starting as new host after migration (participant ID: %02x%02x...)", session->participant_id[0],
2371            session->participant_id[1]);
2372 
2373   // Get configured port (or use default)
2374   int host_port = GET_OPTION(port);
2375 
2376   // Mark ourselves as host
2377   session->is_host = true;
2378 
2379   // Create host context if needed with configured port
2380   if (!session->host_ctx) {
2381     session_host_config_t hconfig = {
2382         .port = host_port,
2383         .ipv4_address = "0.0.0.0",
2384         .max_clients = 32,
2385         .encryption_enabled = true,
2386     };
2387 
2388     session->host_ctx = session_host_create(&hconfig);
2389     if (!session->host_ctx) {
2390       log_error("Failed to create host context for migration");
2391       return ERROR_MEMORY;
2392     }
2393 
2394     // Start listening for new participant connections
2395     asciichat_error_t hstart = session_host_start(session->host_ctx);
2396     if (hstart != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2397       log_error("Failed to start host after migration: %d", hstart);
2398       return hstart;
2399     }
2400 
2401     log_info("Host restarted after migration, listening on port %d", host_port);
2402   }
2403 
2404   // Send HOST_ANNOUNCEMENT to ACDS so other participants can reconnect
2405   acip_host_announcement_t announcement = {0};
2406   memcpy(announcement.session_id, session->session_id, 16);
2407   memcpy(announcement.host_id, session->participant_id, 16);
2408   SAFE_STRNCPY(announcement.host_address, "127.0.0.1", sizeof(announcement.host_address));
2409   // TODO: Use actual determined host address instead of 127.0.0.1
2410   announcement.host_port = host_port;
2411   announcement.connection_type = ACIP_CONNECTION_TYPE_DIRECT_PUBLIC;
2412   // TODO: Use actual connection type based on NAT quality
2413 
2414   if (session->acds_transport) {
2415     packet_send_via_transport(session->acds_transport, PACKET_TYPE_ACIP_HOST_ANNOUNCEMENT, &announcement,
2416                               sizeof(announcement), 0);
2417     log_info("Sent HOST_ANNOUNCEMENT to ACDS for new host %02x%02x... at %s:%u", announcement.host_id[0],
2418              announcement.host_id[1], announcement.host_address, announcement.host_port);
2419   }
2420 
2421   // Mark migration complete
2422   session->migration.state = MIGRATION_STATE_COMPLETE;
2423 
2424   // Transition to ACTIVE state (host is ready)
2425   set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE);
2426 
2427   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
2428 }

asciichat_error_t discovery_session_check_host_alive
  (discovery_session_t * session)


Detect host disconnect (check connection status).


Parameters


Session Session context


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK if host alive, error if disconnected


Definition at line 2241 of file
    src/discovery/session.c.


2241                                                                                    {
2242   if (!session || session->is_host) {
2243     // We are the host, so "host" is always alive
2244     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
2245   }
2246 
2247   // For WebRTC sessions, check the WebRTC transport instead of participant_ctx
2248   if (session->session_type == SESSION_TYPE_WEBRTC) {
2249     // If we have a WebRTC transport and it's marked as ready, host is alive
2250     if (session->webrtc_transport_ready) {
2251       return ASCIICHAT_OK;
2252     }
2253     // WebRTC transport not ready yet - might still be connecting
2254     return ERROR_NETWORK;
2255   }
2256 
2257   // For TCP sessions, check participant context
2258   // If we don't have a participant context yet, we're not connected
2259   if (!session->participant_ctx) {
2260     return ERROR_NETWORK;
2261   }
2262 
2263   // Check migration state - if we're already migrating, host is definitely not alive
2264   if (session->migration.state != MIGRATION_STATE_NONE) {
2265     return ERROR_NETWORK; // Already detected disconnect
2266   }
2267 
2268   uint64_t now_ms = session_get_current_time_ms();
2269 
2270   // Check if we have a ping in flight that timed out
2271   if (session->liveness.ping_in_flight) {
2272     uint64_t ping_age_ms = now_ms - session->liveness.last_ping_sent_ms;
2273     if (ping_age_ms > session->liveness.timeout_ms) {
2274       // Ping timed out
2275       session->liveness.consecutive_failures++;
2276       session->liveness.ping_in_flight = false;
2277       log_warn("Host ping timeout (attempt %u/%u, age=%llu ms)", session->liveness.consecutive_failures,
2278                session->liveness.max_failures, (unsigned long long)ping_age_ms);
2279 
2280       // Check if we've exceeded failure threshold
2281       if (session->liveness.consecutive_failures >= session->liveness.max_failures) {
2282         log_error("Host declared dead after %u consecutive ping failures", session->liveness.consecutive_failures);
2283         return ERROR_NETWORK;
2284       }
2285     }
2286   }
2287 
2288   // Check if it's time to send a new ping
2289   uint64_t time_since_last_ping = now_ms - session->liveness.last_ping_sent_ms;
2290   if (!session->liveness.ping_in_flight && time_since_last_ping >= session->liveness.ping_interval_ms) {
2291     // Send ping to host via participant connection
2292     socket_t host_socket = session_participant_get_socket(session->participant_ctx);
2293     if (host_socket != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
2294       asciichat_error_t result = packet_send(host_socket, PACKET_TYPE_PING, NULL, 0);
2295       if (result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2296         session->liveness.last_ping_sent_ms = now_ms;
2297         session->liveness.ping_in_flight = true;
2298         log_debug("Sent ping to host (attempt %u/%u)", session->liveness.consecutive_failures + 1,
2299                   session->liveness.max_failures);
2300       } else {
2301         log_warn("Failed to send ping to host: %d", result);
2302         session->liveness.consecutive_failures++;
2303         if (session->liveness.consecutive_failures >= session->liveness.max_failures) {
2304           log_error("Host declared dead after %u consecutive send failures", session->liveness.consecutive_failures);
2305           return ERROR_NETWORK;
2306         }
2307       }
2308     }
2309   }
2310 
2311   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
2312 }

Parameters


Session Session context


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success


Definition at line 2430 of file

2430                                                                                          {
2431   if (!session) {
2432     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session is NULL");
2433     return ERROR_INVALID_PARAM;
2434   }
2435 
2436   log_info("Reconnecting to future host: %s:%u (connection type: %u)", session->ring.future_host_address,
2437            session->ring.future_host_port, session->ring.future_host_connection_type);
2438 
2439   // Destroy old participant context if present
2440   if (session->participant_ctx) {
2441     session_participant_destroy(session->participant_ctx);
2442     session->participant_ctx = NULL;
2443   }
2444 
2445   // Create new participant context for the future host
2446   session_participant_config_t pconfig = {
2447       .address = session->ring.future_host_address,
2448       .port = session->ring.future_host_port,
2449       .enable_audio = true,
2450       .enable_video = true,
2451       .encryption_enabled = true,
2452   };
2453 
2454   session->participant_ctx = session_participant_create(&pconfig);
2455   if (!session->participant_ctx) {
2456     log_error("Failed to create participant context for future host");
2457     return ERROR_MEMORY;
2458   }
2459 
2460   // Attempt connection to future host
2461   asciichat_error_t pconn = session_participant_connect(session->participant_ctx);
2462   if (pconn != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2463     log_error("Failed to connect to future host: %d", pconn);
2464     return pconn;
2465   }
2466 
2467   log_info("Connected to future host after migration");
2468 
2469   // Update our host info with the new future host details
2470   memcpy(session->host_id, session->ring.future_host_id, 16);
2471   SAFE_STRNCPY(session->host_address, session->ring.future_host_address, sizeof(session->host_address));
2472   session->host_port = session->ring.future_host_port;
2473 
2474   log_info("Updated host info to: %s:%u (id: %02x%02x...)", session->host_address, session->host_port,
2475            session->host_id[0], session->host_id[1]);
2476 
2477   // Mark migration complete (in real implementation, this would be done after successful connection)
2478   session->migration.state = MIGRATION_STATE_COMPLETE;
2479 
2480   // Transition to ACTIVE state (participant is ready)
2481   set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE);
2482 
2483   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
2484 }

53   if (!config) {
54     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "config is NULL");
55     return NULL;
56   }
57 
58   discovery_session_t *session = SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(discovery_session_t), discovery_session_t *);
59   if (!session) {
60     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate discovery session");
61     return NULL;
62   }
63 
64   session->state = DISCOVERY_STATE_INIT;
65   session->acds_socket = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
66   session->acds_transport = NULL;
67   session->peer_manager = NULL;
68   session->webrtc_transport_ready = false;
69   session->webrtc_connection_initiated = false;
70   session->webrtc_retry_attempt = 0;
71   session->webrtc_last_attempt_time_ms = 0;
72   session->stun_servers = NULL;
73   session->stun_count = 0;
74   session->turn_servers = NULL;
75   session->turn_count = 0;
76 
77   // Initialize host liveness detection
78   session->liveness.last_ping_sent_ms = 0;
79   session->liveness.last_pong_received_ms = 0;
80   session->liveness.consecutive_failures = 0;
81   session->liveness.max_failures = 3;
82   session->liveness.ping_interval_ms = 3 * MS_PER_SEC_INT; // Ping every 3 seconds
83   session->liveness.timeout_ms = 10 * MS_PER_SEC_INT;      // 10 second timeout
84   session->liveness.ping_in_flight = false;
85 
86   log_info("discovery_session_create: After CALLOC - participant_ctx=%p, host_ctx=%p", session->participant_ctx,
87            session->host_ctx);
88 
89   // Load or generate identity key
90   char identity_path[256];
91   asciichat_error_t id_result = acds_identity_default_path(identity_path, sizeof(identity_path));
92   if (id_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
93     id_result = acds_identity_load(identity_path, session->identity_pubkey, session->identity_seckey);
94     if (id_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
95       // File doesn't exist or is corrupted - generate new key
96       log_info("Identity key not found, generating new key");
97       id_result = acds_identity_generate(session->identity_pubkey, session->identity_seckey);
98       if (id_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
99         // Save for future use
100         acds_identity_save(identity_path, session->identity_pubkey, session->identity_seckey);
101       }
102     }
103   }
104 
105   if (id_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
106     log_warn("Failed to load/generate identity key, using zero key");
107     memset(session->identity_pubkey, 0, sizeof(session->identity_pubkey));
108     memset(session->identity_seckey, 0, sizeof(session->identity_seckey));
109   } else {
110     char fingerprint[65];
111     acds_identity_fingerprint(session->identity_pubkey, fingerprint);
112     log_info("Using identity key with fingerprint: %.16s...", fingerprint);
113   }
114 
115   // Load multiple identity keys from options (multi-key protocol support)
116   const options_t *opts = options_get();
117   if (opts && opts->num_identity_keys > 0) {
118     // Load all identity keys from options
119     for (size_t i = 0; i < opts->num_identity_keys && i < MAX_IDENTITY_KEYS; i++) {
120       uint8_t pubkey[32] = {0};
121       uint8_t seckey[64] = {0};
122       asciichat_error_t key_result = acds_identity_load(opts->identity_keys[i], pubkey, seckey);
123       if (key_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
124         memcpy(session->identity_pubkeys[i], pubkey, 32);
125         char fingerprint[65];
126         acds_identity_fingerprint(pubkey, fingerprint);
127         log_info("Loaded identity key #%zu with fingerprint: %.16s...", i + 1, fingerprint);
128       } else {
129         log_warn("Failed to load identity key #%zu from %s", i + 1, opts->identity_keys[i]);
130       }
131     }
132     session->num_identity_keys = opts->num_identity_keys;
133     log_info("Loaded %zu identity key(s) for multi-key protocol", session->num_identity_keys);
134   } else {
135     // Single-key mode: use only the primary identity key
136     memcpy(session->identity_pubkeys[0], session->identity_pubkey, 32);
137     session->num_identity_keys = 1;
138   }
139 
140   // ACDS connection info
141   if (config->acds_url && config->acds_url[0]) {
142     SAFE_STRNCPY(session->acds_url, config->acds_url, sizeof(session->acds_url));
143   }
144   if (config->acds_address && config->acds_address[0]) {
145     SAFE_STRNCPY(session->acds_address, config->acds_address, sizeof(session->acds_address));
146   } else {
147     SAFE_STRNCPY(session->acds_address, "127.0.0.1", sizeof(session->acds_address));
148   }
149   session->acds_port = config->acds_port > 0 ? config->acds_port : ACIP_DISCOVERY_DEFAULT_PORT;
150 
151   // Session string (if joining)
152   if (config->session_string && config->session_string[0]) {
153     SAFE_STRNCPY(session->session_string, config->session_string, sizeof(session->session_string));
154     session->is_initiator = false;
155   } else {
156     session->is_initiator = true;
157   }
158 
159   // Callbacks
160   session->on_state_change = config->on_state_change;
161   session->on_session_ready = config->on_session_ready;
162   session->on_error = config->on_error;
163   session->callback_user_data = config->callback_user_data;
164   session->should_exit_callback = config->should_exit_callback;
165   session->exit_callback_data = config->exit_callback_data;
166 
167   log_debug("Discovery session created (initiator=%d, acds=%s:%u)", session->is_initiator, session->acds_address,
168             session->acds_port);
169 
170   return session;
171 }

Session Session to
  destroy

173                                                              {
174   if (!session) {
175     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session is NULL");
176     return;
177   }
178 
179   // Close ACDS connection
180   if (session->acds_transport) {
181     acip_transport_destroy(session->acds_transport);
182     session->acds_transport = NULL;
183   }
184   if (session->acds_socket != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
185     socket_close(session->acds_socket);
186     session->acds_socket = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
187   }
188 
189   // Destroy host context if active
190   if (session->host_ctx) {
191     session_host_destroy(session->host_ctx);
192     session->host_ctx = NULL;
193   }
194 
195   // Destroy participant context if active
196   if (session->participant_ctx) {
197     session_participant_destroy(session->participant_ctx);
198     session->participant_ctx = NULL;
199   }
200 
201   // Clean up WebRTC peer manager (NEW)
202   if (session->peer_manager) {
203     webrtc_peer_manager_destroy(session->peer_manager);
204     session->peer_manager = NULL;
205   }
206 
207   // Clean up STUN/TURN server arrays (NEW)
208   if (session->stun_servers) {
209     SAFE_FREE(session->stun_servers);
210     session->stun_servers = NULL;
211     session->stun_count = 0;
212   }
213 
214   if (session->turn_servers) {
215     SAFE_FREE(session->turn_servers);
216     session->turn_servers = NULL;
217     session->turn_count = 0;
218   }
219 
220   SAFE_FREE(session);
221 }

  (discovery_session_t * session, uint32_t disconnect_reason)


Handle host disconnect with automatic failover to future host.


Parameters


Session Session context


disconnect_reason Reason code for disconnect


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on successful failover


Definition at line 2314 of file
    src/discovery/session.c.


2314                                                                                                                      {
2315   if (!session) {
2316     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session is NULL");
2317     return ERROR_INVALID_PARAM;
2318   }
2319 
2320   log_warn("Host disconnect detected: reason=%u", disconnect_reason);
2321 
2322   // Initialize migration context
2323   session->migration.state = MIGRATION_STATE_DETECTED;
2324   session->migration.detection_time_ms = session_get_current_time_ms();
2325   memcpy(session->migration.last_host_id, session->host_id, 16);
2326   session->migration.disconnect_reason = disconnect_reason;
2327 
2328   // Notify ACDS of host loss (lightweight notification, no NAT data exchanged!)
2329   acip_host_lost_t host_lost = {0};
2330   memcpy(host_lost.session_id, session->session_id, 16);
2331   memcpy(host_lost.participant_id, session->participant_id, 16);
2332   memcpy(host_lost.last_host_id, session->host_id, 16);
2333   host_lost.disconnect_reason = disconnect_reason;
2334   host_lost.disconnect_time_ms = session->migration.detection_time_ms;
2335 
2336   packet_send_via_transport(session->acds_transport, PACKET_TYPE_ACIP_HOST_LOST, &host_lost, sizeof(host_lost), 0);
2337 
2338   // Check if we have a pre-elected future host (from last 5-minute ring round)
2339   if (session->ring.future_host_id[0] == 0) {
2340     // No future host known! This shouldn't happen if we just received FUTURE_HOST_ELECTED
2341     // within the last 5 minutes. Fall back to treating this as fatal.
2342     log_error("No future host pre-elected! Session cannot recover from host disconnect.");
2343     session->migration.state = MIGRATION_STATE_COMPLETE;
2344     return ERROR_NETWORK; // Session should end
2345   }
2346 
2347   // **SIMPLIFIED**: No metrics exchange needed! Future host was already elected 5 minutes ago.
2348   // Just failover to the pre-elected host.
2349   session->migration.state = MIGRATION_STATE_FAILOVER;
2350 
2351   // Check if I am the future host
2352   if (session->ring.am_future_host) {
2353     // I become the new host immediately! (no election delay!)
2354     log_info("Becoming host (I'm the pre-elected future host)");
2355     return discovery_session_become_host(session);
2356   } else {
2357     // Connect to pre-elected future host (address already stored!)
2358     log_info("Connecting to pre-elected future host: %s:%u", session->ring.future_host_address,
2359              session->ring.future_host_port);
2360     return discovery_session_connect_to_future_host(session);
2361   }
2362 }

asciichat_error_t discovery_session_process
  (discovery_session_t * session, int64_t timeout_ns)


Process session events (call in main loop). Handles incoming ACDS
    messages, negotiation, and state transitions.




Parameters


Session Session context


timeout_ns Max time to wait for events in nanoseconds (0 =
  non-blocking)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error on failure


Definition at line 1519 of file
    src/discovery/session.c.


1519                                                                                               {
1520   if (!session) {
1521     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session is NULL");
1522   }
1523 
1524   // Handle state-specific processing
1525   switch (session->state) {
1526   case DISCOVERY_STATE_WAITING_PEER: {
1527     // Wait for PARTICIPANT_JOINED notification from ACDS
1528     // Track total wait time to avoid infinite blocking in test/snapshot scenarios
1529     uint64_t now_ns = time_get_ns();
1530     uint64_t wait_duration = now_ns - session->waiting_peer_start_ns;
1531     const uint64_t MAX_WAIT_NS = 2000000000;  // 2 second timeout for waiting
1532 
1533     if (wait_duration >= MAX_WAIT_NS) {
1534       // Timeout waiting for peer - proceed as solo host
1535       log_warn("WAITING_PEER timeout after %.2f seconds, proceeding as solo host",
1536                wait_duration / 1e9);
1537       session->is_host = true;  // We become the host since peer didn't join
1538       session->host_elected = true;
1539       set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE);
1540       return ASCIICHAT_OK;
1541     }
1542 
1543     if (session->acds_transport && timeout_ns > 0) {
1544       // Do not set socket timeout - let receive_packet_secure use its own timeout
1545       // Setting a short socket timeout conflicts with its hardcoded 1-second timeout
1546       packet_type_t type;
1547       void *data = NULL;
1548       size_t len = 0;
1549       void *alloc_buffer = NULL;
1550 
1551       // Try to receive a packet
1552       asciichat_error_t recv_result =
1553           packet_receive_via_transport(session->acds_transport, &type, &data, &len, &alloc_buffer);
1554       if (recv_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1555         if (type == PACKET_TYPE_ACIP_PARTICIPANT_JOINED) {
1556           log_info("Peer joined session, transitioning to negotiation");
1557           set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_NEGOTIATING);
1558           buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1559         } else {
1560           // Got some other packet, handle it or ignore
1561           log_debug("Received packet type %d while waiting for peer", type);
1562           buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1563         }
1564       } else if (recv_result == ERROR_NETWORK_TIMEOUT) {
1565         // Timeout is normal, just return
1566       } else {
1567         log_debug("Packet receive error while waiting for peer: %d", recv_result);
1568       }
1569     } else {
1570       // Fallback to sleep if no timeout or invalid transport
1571       platform_sleep_us(timeout_ns / 1000);
1572     }
1573     break;
1574   }
1575 
1576   case DISCOVERY_STATE_NEGOTIATING: {
1577     // Handle NAT negotiation
1578     if (session->negotiate.state == NEGOTIATE_STATE_INIT) {
1579       // Initialize negotiation
1580       negotiate_init(&session->negotiate, session->session_id, session->participant_id, session->is_initiator);
1581       log_info("Starting NAT negotiation (initiator=%d)", session->is_initiator);
1582 
1583       // Send our NETWORK_QUALITY to ACDS immediately (let peer receive it)
1584       asciichat_error_t send_result = send_network_quality_to_acds(session);
1585       if (send_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1586         log_warn("Failed to send initial NETWORK_QUALITY: %d", send_result);
1587         // Don't fail - we'll try again next iteration
1588       }
1589     }
1590 
1591     // Try to receive peer's NETWORK_QUALITY from ACDS (non-blocking with short timeout)
1592     if (!session->negotiate.peer_quality_received) {
1593       asciichat_error_t recv_result = receive_network_quality_from_acds(session);
1594       if (recv_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1595         // Got peer quality - proceed to election
1596         log_info("Received peer NETWORK_QUALITY, proceeding to host election");
1597       } else if (recv_result != ERROR_NETWORK_TIMEOUT) {
1598         // Real error (not just timeout)
1599         log_debug("Error receiving NETWORK_QUALITY: %d (this might be normal if peer not ready)", recv_result);
1600       }
1601     }
1602 
1603     // Check if we have both qualities and can determine host
1604     if (session->negotiate.peer_quality_received && session->negotiate.state != NEGOTIATE_STATE_COMPARING) {
1605       // Both sides have exchanged NAT quality - determine the winner
1606       log_info("Both NAT qualities received, determining host...");
1607       asciichat_error_t result = negotiate_determine_result(&session->negotiate);
1608       if (result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1609         // Store result
1610         session->is_host = session->negotiate.we_are_host;
1611 
1612         if (session->is_host) {
1613           // We won the negotiation - become the host
1614           log_info("🏠 NAT negotiation complete: WE ARE HOST (tier:%d vs tier:%d)",
1615                    nat_compute_tier(&session->negotiate.our_quality),
1616                    nat_compute_tier(&session->negotiate.peer_quality));
1617           memcpy(session->host_id, session->participant_id, 16);
1618           SAFE_STRNCPY(session->host_address, session->negotiate.host_address, sizeof(session->host_address));
1619           session->host_port = session->negotiate.host_port;
1620           set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_STARTING_HOST);
1621         } else {
1622           // They won - we'll be a participant
1623           log_info("👤 NAT negotiation complete: THEY ARE HOST at %s:%u (tier:%d vs tier:%d)",
1624                    session->negotiate.host_address, session->negotiate.host_port,
1625                    nat_compute_tier(&session->negotiate.our_quality),
1626                    nat_compute_tier(&session->negotiate.peer_quality));
1627           SAFE_STRNCPY(session->host_address, session->negotiate.host_address, sizeof(session->host_address));
1628           session->host_port = session->negotiate.host_port;
1629           set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_CONNECTING_HOST);
1630         }
1631       } else {
1632         log_error("Failed to determine negotiation result: %d", result);
1633         set_error(session, result, "Host election failed");
1634       }
1635     } else {
1636       // Still waiting for peer quality - sleep briefly to avoid busy loop
1637       platform_sleep_us(timeout_ns / 1000);
1638     }
1639     break;
1640   }
1641 
1642   case DISCOVERY_STATE_STARTING_HOST: {
1643     // Start hosting
1644     if (!session->host_ctx) {
1645       // Create host context with configured port
1646       int host_port = GET_OPTION(port);
1647 
1648       session_host_config_t hconfig = {
1649           .port = host_port,
1650           .ipv4_address = "0.0.0.0",
1651           .max_clients = 32,
1652           .encryption_enabled = true,
1653       };
1654 
1655       session->host_ctx = session_host_create(&hconfig);
1656       if (!session->host_ctx) {
1657         log_error("Failed to create host context");
1658         set_error(session, ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create host context");
1659         break;
1660       }
1661 
1662       // Start listening
1663       asciichat_error_t hstart = session_host_start(session->host_ctx);
1664       if (hstart != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1665         log_error("Failed to start host: %d", hstart);
1666         set_error(session, hstart, "Failed to start host");
1667         break;
1668       }
1669 
1670       log_info("Host started, listening for connections");
1671     }
1672 
1673     // Notify listeners that we're ready
1674     if (session->on_session_ready) {
1675       session->on_session_ready(session->session_string, session->callback_user_data);
1676     }
1677 
1678     set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE);
1679     break;
1680   }
1681 
1682   case DISCOVERY_STATE_CONNECTING_HOST: {
1683     // Connect to host as participant
1684 
1685     // For WebRTC sessions, wait for DataChannel to open
1686     if (session->session_type == SESSION_TYPE_WEBRTC) {
1687       // Check if transport is ready (DataChannel opened)
1688       if (session->webrtc_transport_ready) {
1689         log_info("WebRTC DataChannel established, transitioning to ACTIVE state");
1690         set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE);
1691         break;
1692       }
1693 
1694       // If not ready and we haven't initiated connection yet, initiate it
1695       if (session->peer_manager && !session->webrtc_connection_initiated) {
1696         log_info("Initiating WebRTC connection to host (attempt %d/%d)...", session->webrtc_retry_attempt + 1,
1697                  GET_OPTION(webrtc_reconnect_attempts) + 1);
1698 
1699         // Initiate connection (generates offer, triggers SDP exchange)
1700         asciichat_error_t conn_result =
1701             webrtc_peer_manager_connect(session->peer_manager, session->session_id, session->host_id);
1702 
1703         if (conn_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1704           log_error("Failed to initiate WebRTC connection: %d", conn_result);
1705           set_error(session, conn_result, "Failed to initiate WebRTC connection");
1706           break;
1707         }
1708 
1709         log_info("WebRTC connection initiated, waiting for DataChannel...");
1710         session->webrtc_connection_initiated = true;
1711         session->webrtc_last_attempt_time_ms = platform_get_monotonic_time_us() / 1000;
1712       }
1713 
1714       // Check for ICE gathering timeout on all peers
1715       if (session->webrtc_connection_initiated && session->peer_manager) {
1716         int timeout_ms = GET_OPTION(webrtc_ice_timeout_ms);
1717         int timed_out_count = webrtc_peer_manager_check_gathering_timeouts(session->peer_manager, timeout_ms);
1718 
1719         if (timed_out_count > 0) {
1720           log_error("ICE gathering timeout: %d peer(s) failed to gather candidates within %dms", timed_out_count,
1721                     timeout_ms);
1722 
1723           // Check if we have retries remaining
1724           int max_attempts = GET_OPTION(webrtc_reconnect_attempts);
1725           if (session->webrtc_retry_attempt < max_attempts) {
1726             // Calculate exponential backoff delay
1727             uint32_t backoff_ms = calculate_backoff_delay_ms(session->webrtc_retry_attempt);
1728 
1729             log_warn("WebRTC connection attempt %d/%d failed, retrying in %ums...", session->webrtc_retry_attempt + 1,
1730                      max_attempts + 1, backoff_ms);
1731 
1732             // Wait for backoff period
1733             platform_sleep_ms(backoff_ms);
1734 
1735             // Destroy old peer manager
1736             webrtc_peer_manager_destroy(session->peer_manager);
1737             session->peer_manager = NULL;
1738 
1739             // Re-initialize peer manager for retry
1740             asciichat_error_t reinit_result = initialize_webrtc_peer_manager(session);
1741             if (reinit_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1742               log_error("Failed to re-initialize WebRTC for retry: %d", reinit_result);
1743               set_error(session, reinit_result, "Failed to re-initialize WebRTC for retry");
1744 
1745               // If --prefer-webrtc is set, this is a fatal error
1746               if (GET_OPTION(prefer_webrtc)) {
1747                 log_fatal("WebRTC connection failed and --prefer-webrtc is set - exiting");
1748                 set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_FAILED);
1749                 return ERROR_NETWORK_TIMEOUT;
1750               }
1751 
1752               break; // Exit this case to allow fallback to TCP
1753             }
1754 
1755             // Reset connection state for retry
1756             session->webrtc_connection_initiated = false;
1757             session->webrtc_retry_attempt++;
1758 
1759             log_info("WebRTC peer manager re-initialized for attempt %d/%d", session->webrtc_retry_attempt + 1,
1760                      max_attempts + 1);
1761           } else {
1762             // No more retries - connection failed
1763             log_error("WebRTC connection failed after %d attempts (timeout: %dms)", max_attempts + 1, timeout_ms);
1764 
1765             // Set error state - WebRTC connection failed
1766             char error_msg[256];
1767             snprintf(error_msg, sizeof(error_msg), "WebRTC connection failed after %d attempts (%dms timeout)",
1768                      max_attempts + 1, timeout_ms);
1769             set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK_TIMEOUT, error_msg);
1770 
1771             // If --prefer-webrtc is set, this is a fatal error (no TCP fallback)
1772             if (GET_OPTION(prefer_webrtc)) {
1773               log_fatal("WebRTC connection failed and --prefer-webrtc is set - exiting");
1774               set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_FAILED);
1775               return ERROR_NETWORK_TIMEOUT;
1776             }
1777 
1778             break; // Exit this case to allow fallback to TCP
1779           }
1780         }
1781       }
1782 
1783       // Poll ACDS for WebRTC signaling messages (SDP answer, ICE candidates)
1784       // while waiting for DataChannel to open
1785       if (session->acds_transport) {
1786         // Check if data available (non-blocking with timeout_ns)
1787         struct timeval tv_timeout;
1788         tv_timeout.tv_sec = timeout_ns / NS_PER_SEC_INT;
1789         tv_timeout.tv_usec = (timeout_ns % NS_PER_SEC_INT) / 1000;
1790 
1791         fd_set readfds;
1792         FD_ZERO(&readfds);
1793         FD_SET(session->acds_socket, &readfds);
1794 
1795         int select_result = select(session->acds_socket + 1, &readfds, NULL, NULL, &tv_timeout);
1796         if (select_result > 0 && FD_ISSET(session->acds_socket, &readfds)) {
1797           // Data available to read
1798           packet_type_t type;
1799           void *data = NULL;
1800           size_t len = 0;
1801           void *alloc_buffer = NULL;
1802 
1803           asciichat_error_t recv_result =
1804               packet_receive_via_transport(session->acds_transport, &type, &data, &len, &alloc_buffer);
1805 
1806           if (recv_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1807             // Dispatch to appropriate handler
1808             handle_acds_webrtc_packet(session, type, data, len);
1809             buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1810           } else {
1811             log_debug("Failed to receive ACDS packet: %d", recv_result);
1812           }
1813         }
1814       }
1815 
1816       // Stay in CONNECTING_HOST until DataChannel opens (webrtc_transport_ready becomes true)
1817       // The discovery_on_transport_ready callback will set webrtc_transport_ready=true
1818       break;
1819     }
1820 
1821     if (!session->participant_ctx) {
1822       // Create participant context for this session
1823       session_participant_config_t pconfig = {
1824           .address = session->host_address,
1825           .port = session->host_port,
1826           .enable_audio = true,
1827           .enable_video = true,
1828           .encryption_enabled = true,
1829       };
1830 
1831       session->participant_ctx = session_participant_create(&pconfig);
1832       if (!session->participant_ctx) {
1833         log_error("Failed to create participant context");
1834         set_error(session, ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create participant context");
1835         break;
1836       }
1837 
1838       // Attempt connection
1839       asciichat_error_t pconn = session_participant_connect(session->participant_ctx);
1840       if (pconn != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1841         log_error("Failed to connect as participant: %d", pconn);
1842         // Stay in this state, will retry on next process() call
1843         break;
1844       }
1845 
1846       log_info("Connected to host, performing crypto handshake...");
1847 
1848       // Perform crypto handshake with server
1849       // Get the socket from participant context
1850       socket_t participant_socket = session_participant_get_socket(session->participant_ctx);
1851       if (participant_socket == INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
1852         log_error("Failed to get socket from participant context");
1853         set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to get participant socket");
1854         session_participant_disconnect(session->participant_ctx);
1855         break;
1856       }
1857 
1858       // Step 1: Send client protocol version
1859       log_debug("Sending protocol version to server...");
1860       protocol_version_packet_t client_version = {0};
1861       client_version.protocol_version = HOST_TO_NET_U16(1);  // Protocol version 1
1862       client_version.protocol_revision = HOST_TO_NET_U16(0); // Revision 0
1863       client_version.supports_encryption = 1;                // We support encryption
1864       client_version.compression_algorithms = 0;
1865       client_version.compression_threshold = 0;
1866       client_version.feature_flags = 0;
1867 
1868       int result = send_protocol_version_packet(participant_socket, &client_version);
1869       if (result != 0) {
1870         log_error("Failed to send protocol version to server");
1871         set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to send protocol version");
1872         session_participant_disconnect(session->participant_ctx);
1873         break;
1874       }
1875 
1876       // Step 2: Receive server protocol version
1877       log_debug("Receiving server protocol version...");
1878       packet_type_t packet_type;
1879       void *payload = NULL;
1880       size_t payload_len = 0;
1881 
1882       int recv_result = receive_packet(participant_socket, &packet_type, &payload, &payload_len);
1883       if (recv_result != ASCIICHAT_OK || packet_type != PACKET_TYPE_PROTOCOL_VERSION) {
1884         log_error("Failed to receive server protocol version (got type 0x%x)", packet_type);
1885         if (payload) {
1886           buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_len);
1887         }
1888         set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to receive protocol version from server");
1889         session_participant_disconnect(session->participant_ctx);
1890         break;
1891       }
1892 
1893       if (payload_len != sizeof(protocol_version_packet_t)) {
1894         log_error("Invalid protocol version packet size: %zu", payload_len);
1895         buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_len);
1896         set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK, "Invalid protocol version packet");
1897         session_participant_disconnect(session->participant_ctx);
1898         break;
1899       }
1900 
1901       protocol_version_packet_t server_version;
1902       memcpy(&server_version, payload, sizeof(protocol_version_packet_t));
1903       buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_len);
1904 
1905       if (!server_version.supports_encryption) {
1906         log_error("Server does not support encryption");
1907         set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK, "Server does not support encryption");
1908         session_participant_disconnect(session->participant_ctx);
1909         break;
1910       }
1911 
1912       log_info("Server protocol version: %u.%u (encryption: yes)", NET_TO_HOST_U16(server_version.protocol_version),
1913                NET_TO_HOST_U16(server_version.protocol_revision));
1914 
1915       // Step 3: Send crypto capabilities
1916       log_debug("Sending crypto capabilities...");
1917       crypto_capabilities_packet_t client_caps = {0};
1918       client_caps.supported_kex_algorithms = HOST_TO_NET_U16(0x0001);    // KEX_ALGO_X25519
1919       client_caps.supported_auth_algorithms = HOST_TO_NET_U16(0x0003);   // AUTH_ALGO_ED25519 | AUTH_ALGO_NONE
1920       client_caps.supported_cipher_algorithms = HOST_TO_NET_U16(0x0001); // CIPHER_ALGO_XSALSA20_POLY1305
1921       client_caps.requires_verification = 0;
1922       client_caps.preferred_kex = 0x0001;    // KEX_ALGO_X25519
1923       client_caps.preferred_auth = 0x0001;   // AUTH_ALGO_ED25519
1924       client_caps.preferred_cipher = 0x0001; // CIPHER_ALGO_XSALSA20_POLY1305
1925 
1926       result = send_crypto_capabilities_packet(participant_socket, &client_caps);
1927       if (result != 0) {
1928         log_error("Failed to send crypto capabilities");
1929         set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to send crypto capabilities");
1930         session_participant_disconnect(session->participant_ctx);
1931         break;
1932       }
1933 
1934       log_info("Crypto handshake initiated successfully");
1935       log_warn("*** Connected to host as participant - transitioning to ACTIVE ***");
1936     }
1937 
1938     // Check if connection is established
1939     bool ctx_valid = session->participant_ctx != NULL;
1940     bool is_connected = ctx_valid ? session_participant_is_connected(session->participant_ctx) : false;
1941     log_debug("discovery_session_process: CONNECTING_HOST - participant_ctx=%p, ctx_valid=%d, is_connected=%d",
1942               session->participant_ctx, ctx_valid, is_connected);
1943 
1944     if (ctx_valid && !is_connected) {
1945       // Log more details about why it's not connected
1946       log_debug("discovery_session_process: CONNECTING_HOST - context exists but not connected yet");
1947     }
1948 
1949     if (is_connected) {
1950       log_info("Participant connection confirmed, transitioning to ACTIVE state");
1951       set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE);
1952     } else {
1953       log_debug("discovery_session_process: CONNECTING_HOST - awaiting connection establishment (ctx_valid=%d, "
1954                 "is_connected=%d)",
1955                 ctx_valid, is_connected);
1956     }
1957     break;
1958   }
1959 
1960   case DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE:
1961     // Receive and handle ACDS packets for WebRTC signaling (NEW)
1962     if (session->session_type == SESSION_TYPE_WEBRTC && session->acds_transport) {
1963       // Use non-blocking polling to check for incoming ACDS packets
1964       struct pollfd pfd = {.fd = session->acds_socket, .events = POLLIN, .revents = 0};
1965 
1966       int poll_result = socket_poll(&pfd, 1, 0); // Non-blocking poll (timeout=0)
1967 
1968       if (poll_result > 0 && (pfd.revents & POLLIN)) {
1969         // Data available to read
1970         packet_type_t type;
1971         void *data = NULL;
1972         size_t len = 0;
1973         void *alloc_buffer = NULL;
1974 
1975         asciichat_error_t recv_result =
1976             packet_receive_via_transport(session->acds_transport, &type, &data, &len, &alloc_buffer);
1977 
1978         if (recv_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1979           // Dispatch to appropriate handler
1980           handle_acds_webrtc_packet(session, type, data, len);
1981           buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1982         }
1983       }
1984     }
1985 
1986     // Session is active - check for host disconnect
1987     if (!session->is_host) {
1988       // We are a participant - check if host is still alive
1989       asciichat_error_t host_check = discovery_session_check_host_alive(session);
1990       if (host_check != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1991         // Host is down! Trigger automatic failover to pre-elected future host
1992         log_warn("Host disconnect detected during ACTIVE state");
1993 
1994         // If --prefer-webrtc is set, WebRTC failure is fatal (no TCP fallback)
1995         const options_t *opts = options_get();
1996         if (opts && opts->prefer_webrtc) {
1997           log_fatal("WebRTC connection failed and --prefer-webrtc is set - exiting");
1998           set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_FAILED);
1999           session->error = ERROR_NETWORK;
2000           return ERROR_NETWORK;
2001         }
2002 
2003         set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_MIGRATING);
2004         // Reason code: 1 = timeout/disconnect detected during ACTIVE session
2005         discovery_session_handle_host_disconnect(session, 1);
2006       }
2007     }
2008     platform_sleep_us(timeout_ns / 1000);
2009     break;
2010 
2011   case DISCOVERY_STATE_MIGRATING:
2012     // Host migration in progress
2013     // Check if migration has timed out (if it takes >30 seconds, give up)
2014     if (session->migration.state == MIGRATION_STATE_COMPLETE) {
2015       // Migration completed successfully
2016       set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE);
2017       log_info("Migration complete, session resumed");
2018     } else if (session_get_current_time_ms() - session->migration.detection_time_ms > 30 * MS_PER_SEC_INT) {
2019       // Migration timed out after 30 seconds
2020       log_error("Host migration timeout - session cannot recover");
2021       set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_FAILED);
2022       session->error = ERROR_NETWORK;
2023     }
2024     platform_sleep_us(timeout_ns / 1000);
2025     break;
2026 
2027   default:
2028     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Invalid session state");
2029     break;
2030   }
2031 
2032   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
2033 }

asciichat_error_t discovery_session_start
  (discovery_session_t * session)


Start the discovery session. Connects to ACDS and either creates
    or joins a session.




Parameters


Session Session context


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success


Definition at line 1330 of file
    src/discovery/session.c.


1330                                                                         {
1331   log_info("discovery_session_start: ENTRY - session=%p", session);
1332 
1333   if (!session) {
1334     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session is NULL");
1335   }
1336 
1337   log_info("discovery_session_start: is_initiator=%d, session_string='%s'", session->is_initiator,
1338            session->session_string);
1339 
1340   // Check if we should exit before starting blocking operations
1341   if (session->should_exit_callback && session->should_exit_callback(session->exit_callback_data)) {
1342     log_info("discovery_session_start: Exiting early due to should_exit_callback (before ACDS connect)");
1343     return ASCIICHAT_OK; // Return success to allow clean shutdown
1344   }
1345 
1346   log_info("discovery_session_start: Calling connect_to_acds...");
1347   // Connect to ACDS
1348   asciichat_error_t result = connect_to_acds(session);
1349   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1350     log_error("discovery_session_start: connect_to_acds FAILED - result=%d", result);
1351     return result;
1352   }
1353   log_info("discovery_session_start: connect_to_acds succeeded");
1354 
1355   // Check again after connection
1356   if (session->should_exit_callback && session->should_exit_callback(session->exit_callback_data)) {
1357     log_info("discovery_session_start: Exiting early due to should_exit_callback (after ACDS connect)");
1358     return ASCIICHAT_OK; // Return success to allow clean shutdown
1359   }
1360 
1361   // Perform crypto handshake with ACDS (same as client-server handshake)
1362   log_info("discovery_session_start: Performing crypto handshake with ACDS...");
1363   crypto_handshake_context_t handshake_ctx;
1364   memset(&handshake_ctx, 0, sizeof(handshake_ctx));
1365 
1366   result = crypto_handshake_init("discovery_client", &handshake_ctx, false);
1367   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1368     log_error("discovery_session_start: Handshake init failed - %s", asciichat_error_string(result));
1369     set_error(session, result, "Crypto handshake init failed");
1370     return result;
1371   }
1372 
1373   // Store identity key in handshake context
1374   handshake_ctx.client_public_key.type = KEY_TYPE_ED25519;
1375   handshake_ctx.client_private_key.type = KEY_TYPE_ED25519;
1376   memcpy(handshake_ctx.client_public_key.key, session->identity_pubkey, 32);
1377   memcpy(handshake_ctx.client_private_key.key.ed25519, session->identity_seckey, 64);
1378 
1379   // Step 0: Receive CRYPTO_PARAMETERS from ACDS
1380   {
1381     packet_type_t pkt_type;
1382     void *pkt_data = NULL;
1383     size_t pkt_len = 0;
1384     void *alloc_buffer = NULL;
1385 
1386     result = packet_receive_via_transport(session->acds_transport, &pkt_type, &pkt_data, &pkt_len, &alloc_buffer);
1387     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK || pkt_type != PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_PARAMETERS) {
1388       log_error("discovery_session_start: Failed to receive CRYPTO_PARAMETERS (got type %u)", pkt_type);
1389       buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1390       set_error(session, result != ASCIICHAT_OK ? result : ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL,
1391                 "Failed to receive CRYPTO_PARAMETERS");
1392       return result != ASCIICHAT_OK ? result : ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL;
1393     }
1394 
1395     if (pkt_len != sizeof(crypto_parameters_packet_t)) {
1396       log_error("discovery_session_start: Invalid CRYPTO_PARAMETERS size: %zu (expected %zu)", pkt_len,
1397                 sizeof(crypto_parameters_packet_t));
1398       buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1399       set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL, "Invalid CRYPTO_PARAMETERS size");
1400       return ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL;
1401     }
1402 
1403     crypto_parameters_packet_t server_params;
1404     memcpy(&server_params, pkt_data, sizeof(crypto_parameters_packet_t));
1405     buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1406 
1407     // Pass params in network byte order — crypto_handshake_set_parameters
1408     // handles conversion internally for client (is_server=false)
1409     result = crypto_handshake_set_parameters(&handshake_ctx, &server_params);
1410     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1411       log_error("discovery_session_start: Failed to set crypto parameters");
1412       set_error(session, result, "Failed to set crypto parameters");
1413       return result;
1414     }
1415     log_info("discovery_session_start: CRYPTO_PARAMETERS received and applied");
1416   }
1417 
1418   // Step 1: Key exchange - receive KEY_EXCHANGE_INIT and respond
1419   {
1420     packet_type_t pkt_type;
1421     void *pkt_data = NULL;
1422     size_t pkt_len = 0;
1423     void *alloc_buffer = NULL;
1424 
1425     result = packet_receive_via_transport(session->acds_transport, &pkt_type, &pkt_data, &pkt_len, &alloc_buffer);
1426     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1427       log_error("discovery_session_start: Failed to receive KEY_EXCHANGE_INIT");
1428       set_error(session, result, "Handshake key exchange recv failed");
1429       return result;
1430     }
1431 
1432     result = crypto_handshake_client_key_exchange(&handshake_ctx, session->acds_transport, pkt_type, pkt_data, pkt_len);
1433     buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1434 
1435     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1436       log_error("discovery_session_start: Handshake key exchange failed");
1437       set_error(session, result, "Handshake key exchange failed");
1438       return result;
1439     }
1440   }
1441 
1442   // Step 2: Auth response - receive AUTH_CHALLENGE and respond
1443   {
1444     packet_type_t pkt_type;
1445     void *pkt_data = NULL;
1446     size_t pkt_len = 0;
1447     void *alloc_buffer = NULL;
1448 
1449     result = packet_receive_via_transport(session->acds_transport, &pkt_type, &pkt_data, &pkt_len, &alloc_buffer);
1450     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1451       log_error("discovery_session_start: Failed to receive AUTH_CHALLENGE");
1452       set_error(session, result, "Handshake auth response recv failed");
1453       return result;
1454     }
1455 
1456     result =
1457         crypto_handshake_client_auth_response(&handshake_ctx, session->acds_transport, pkt_type, pkt_data, pkt_len);
1458     buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1459 
1460     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1461       log_error("discovery_session_start: Handshake auth response failed");
1462       set_error(session, result, "Handshake auth response failed");
1463       return result;
1464     }
1465   }
1466 
1467   // Step 3: Complete handshake
1468   // Note: Some servers (like ACDS without auth) complete the handshake in step 2,
1469   // so check if we're already done before trying to receive another packet
1470   if (handshake_ctx.state != CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_READY) {
1471     packet_type_t pkt_type;
1472     void *pkt_data = NULL;
1473     size_t pkt_len = 0;
1474     void *alloc_buffer = NULL;
1475 
1476     result = packet_receive_via_transport(session->acds_transport, &pkt_type, &pkt_data, &pkt_len, &alloc_buffer);
1477     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1478       log_error("discovery_session_start: Failed to receive HANDSHAKE_COMPLETE");
1479       set_error(session, result, "Handshake complete recv failed");
1480       return result;
1481     }
1482 
1483     result = crypto_handshake_client_complete(&handshake_ctx, session->acds_transport, pkt_type, pkt_data, pkt_len);
1484     buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1485 
1486     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1487       log_error("discovery_session_start: Handshake complete failed");
1488       set_error(session, result, "Handshake complete failed");
1489       return result;
1490     }
1491   } else {
1492     log_info("discovery_session_start: Handshake already complete from auth_response step");
1493   }
1494 
1495   log_info("discovery_session_start: Crypto handshake with ACDS completed");
1496 
1497   // Check again after handshake
1498   if (session->should_exit_callback && session->should_exit_callback(session->exit_callback_data)) {
1499     log_info("discovery_session_start: Exiting early due to should_exit_callback (after handshake)");
1500     return ASCIICHAT_OK; // Return success to allow clean shutdown
1501   }
1502 
1503   // Create or join session
1504   if (session->is_initiator) {
1505     log_info("discovery_session_start: Calling create_session - participant_ctx before=%p", session->participant_ctx);
1506     result = create_session(session);
1507     log_info("discovery_session_start: create_session returned - participant_ctx after=%p, result=%d",
1508              session->participant_ctx, result);
1509     return result;
1510   } else {
1511     log_info("discovery_session_start: Calling join_session - participant_ctx before=%p", session->participant_ctx);
1512     asciichat_error_t result = join_session(session);
1513     log_info("discovery_session_start: join_session returned - participant_ctx after=%p, state=%d, result=%d",
1514              session->participant_ctx, session->state, result);
1515     return result;
1516   }
1517 }

    ASCIICHAT_OK, buffer_pool_free(),

    crypto_handshake_client_auth_response(),
    crypto_handshake_client_complete(),
    crypto_handshake_client_key_exchange(),
    crypto_handshake_init(), CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_READY,

    ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL,
